evals.name: swiftui-toolpicker-wrapper
evals.prompt: I have a SwiftUI drawing screen that wraps PKCanvasView in UIViewRepresentable. The tool picker appears, but changing tools in the picker does not change what the canvas draws. Give me a corrected wrapper and explain the important PencilKit setup details. Keep it as implementation guidance and Swift snippets only.
evals.expected_output: Explains and demonstrates a SwiftUI PKCanvasView wrapper that registers the canvas as a PKToolPicker observer, makes the canvas first responder when showing the picker, avoids drawing-binding update loops, and uses an appropriate drawing policy.
expectations: Registers the PKCanvasView with PKToolPicker using addObserver(canvas) or equivalent.
Calls becomeFirstResponder when the picker should be visible.
Avoids infinite SwiftUI update loops by comparing the canvas drawing with the bound drawing before assignment.
Explains that .default drawing policy is system Pencil-only-setting aware rather than simply finger-scrolls/pencil-draws.

evals.name: content-version-compatibility
evals.prompt: I'm syncing PKDrawing files between a new iPad drawing app and older iPadOS installs. I want current PencilKit inks when possible, but I need a safe compatibility plan. What should I check or configure before saving and loading drawings?
evals.expected_output: Provides a PencilKit compatibility plan using PKDrawing dataRepresentation/init(data:), requiredContentVersion, PKContentVersion meanings, and maximumSupportedContentVersion on both PKCanvasView and PKToolPicker when restricting features.
expectations: Uses PKDrawing.dataRepresentation() for persistence and PKDrawing(data:) for loading.
Checks requiredContentVersion before syncing or loading drawings across older OS versions.
States that maximumSupportedContentVersion should be set on both PKCanvasView and PKToolPicker when restricting compatibility.

Maps modern content versions accurately enough: version1 for older marker/pen/pencil content, version2 for iPadOS 17 inks including monoline/fountain pen/watercolor/crayon, and later versions for barrel-roll/Reed Pen-era features.

evals.name: paperkit-boundary-routing
evals.prompt: A document editor needs Apple Pencil freehand notes, signatures, shapes, arrows, text boxes, images, stickers, and a standard markup toolbar on iOS 26. Should I build this with PencilKit directly, PaperKit, or both? Give me the framework boundary and the smallest useful code sketch.
evals.expected_output: Routes freeform-only drawing to PencilKit and full markup/document annotation to PaperKit, while explaining the PencilKit/PaperKit integration boundary without expanding PencilKit into PaperKit's full domain.
expectations: Chooses PaperKit for shapes, text boxes, images, stickers, loupes, and a system-standard markup toolbar on iOS 26.
Keeps PencilKit positioned as the direct freeform drawing, PKCanvasView, PKToolPicker, and PKDrawing serialization layer.
Mentions that PaperMarkupViewController uses PKTool/PencilKit drawing tools or that PaperMarkup can append a PKDrawing.
Does not recommend rebuilding PaperKit-style markup elements manually in PencilKit when PaperKit is available.
